In cases where the net take home pay is reduced to amounts below the minimum, deductions shall be prioritized in the order of preference stated in the Annual GAA to comply with the net take home pay of at least P3,000.00.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 None.
observations.reason: The City Accountant does not want to antagonize his co-employees in humanitarian reasons.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Accountant and the heads of Departments enjoin all officials and employees to observe COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and to enforce the appropriate administrative and/or legal sanctions provided under said COA circular.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 As of Dec. 31, 2016, unliquidated travel advances from 2012 amounting to P312,497.90 and 2011 accountability of deceased staff remained.
observations.reason: No sanctions were imposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management desist from charging to government funds the expenses for the Miss Tanjay beauty pageant and other expenses such as for fireworks and TV coverage during celebration of events like the city’s Charter Anniversary and fiesta.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The city already stopped charging to government funds the beauty pageant and related expenses starting CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management give priority to the funding of its Solid Waste Management Program and the construction of a sanitary landfill, possibly through surplus and reversion, to establish a sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 In Dec. 2016 all appropriations for construction were reverted to a loan.
observations.reason: The city still has funds to raise for the construction of a sanitary landfill and has reverted funds to loans.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accountant assign additional personnel for the sorting and preparation of vouchers to be submitted to the auditor for post-audit and to monitor and ensure the timely preparation and submission of the bank reconciliation statements, the quarterly trial balances and transaction documents within the reglementary periods.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The submission is still delayed by at least 5 months to a maximum of 8 months.
observations.reason: The City Treasurer does not submit the receipts to the Accountant’s Office within the deadline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the General Services Officer and the City Accountant to reconcile the physical count report from the GSO and the Accounting Office, to identify the persons accountable therefor and make them answer for the properties lost.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The GSO has determined that the variance was due to inexistent records of movable properties for the years prior to the creation of the General Services Office. The GSO is still to request the Accountant for a copy of all entries for movable properties.
observations.reason: The Accountant is still waiting for the 2013 inventory report submitted by the GSO. The GSO is requesting the Accountant for a copy of all entries for movable properties. Coordination between the two offices is needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit an explanation for the irregularities observed specifically the splitting of projects and lack of public bidding, and submit the following supporting documents: i. Location plan for each project, specifying the structure/facility to be painted or improved; ii. Scope of work for each structure or facility; iii. Plans/drawings; iv. Explanation for the absence of data in the canvass. Henceforth, consolidate procurement of similar materials and conduct public bidding to include major suppliers in Dumaguete City.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The City Engineer stated that the projects were carefully crafted not to exceed the P300,000.00 limit of procurement under R.A. 9184 or the Government Procurement Reform Act. The GSO explained that bulk procurement of construction materials is now done through public bidding.
observations.reason: Not acted upon by the City Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit explanations for the issues raised, specifically the excessive quantities of office supplies purchased, the lack of public bidding, the participation of only four suppliers in each procurement, three of whom had no business establishment. Submit proof of consumption of inks and toners by submitting Reports of Waste Materials together with the empty cartridges to the General Services Office for inspection by the auditor. Henceforth procure office supplies through public bidding and submit copies of all purchase orders to COA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Explanations were submitted. Copy of purchase orders submitted weekly. The GSO has started the recording of purchases of supplies to ledger cards. Procurement of office supplies is now done through public bidding.
observations.reason: No reason given.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of hiring employees on job order basis and instead issue casual appointments. Henceforth, always observe the required Personal Services limitation pursuant to Sec. 325(a) of R.A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 In CY 2016 the number of job order employees rose to 1,523 from 1,352 in CY 2015. The city still hires job order personnel as a means of giving employment.
observations.reason: The City Mayor considers this practice as a means of giving employment and means of living to poor constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management closely monitor and vigorously pursue the timely and full implementation of all the programs and activities embodied in the 20% development fund budget which are considered necessary and to immediately benefit the intended beneficiaries. Management should also review the unimplemented programs, projects and activities, then propose for reversion those undertakings that are no longer doable.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 In CY 2016 the number of projects implemented increased; some continuing appropriations were utilized in CY 2016.
observations.reason: CY 2016 was an election year so implementation of projects was affected by the election ban. Moreover, there was a change of administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to bill the COMELEC for the amount disbursed by the city as honoraria to the Board of Inspectors in the May 14, 2007 National and Local Elections, otherwise, require the recipients to refund the amount collected.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2007 The city already sent a bill to the COMELEC as evidenced by Post Office Registry Receipt for Package No. 1136 dated 03/21/2009.
observations.reason: Waiting for COMELEC action on the billed amount.

observations.recommendation: Require the City Treasurer to lay down the requirements for the data and reports that need to be generated by their office then require the supplier to install the programs for generating the specified computerized reports.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2007 The Accounting Office has started the recording of supplies to ledger cards. The CTO requested upgrade; the existing collection system is running very slow and needs to be upgraded. She requested funding amounting to 5M and is now awaiting approval.
observations.reason: The existing collection system is very slow and needs upgrade. The CTO is looking for another supplier and requested funding.

observations.recommendation: Moreover, secure the Transfer Certificate of Title to the lot purchased as an indispensable requirement. Secure the detailed report from the Provincial Appraisal Committee (PAC) which was used as the basis for the said valuation, including BIR zonal valuation and commercial banks' valuation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2005 A copy of the title has been located in CY 2016 so the City can now proceed with the transfer of title.
observations.reason: The title of the previous owner was misplaced so the City could not process the transfer earlier; a copy of the title has been located in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Treasurer to prepare a status report of the loan remittances made by the members of the BALT I as contained in the MOA. If no payments have been received, direct the City Treasurer to issue demand letters requiring payment with coordination from the CPDC. Subsequently, all similar MOAs should invariably include provisions for the imposition of penalties in case of default.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2004 BALT I already paid the amount of loan in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Require the City Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards and the General Services Officer to maintain stock cards, respectively, for all items subject to inventory as required in the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2003 The City Accounting Office has started the recording of purchases of supplies to ledger cards. GSO still has to start the recording to stock cards.
observations.reason: GSO still has to start the recording to stock cards.
